Summary:
Quick Optimize walkthrough focused on adaptive lookahead (prefix / KV cache) + batching recommendations tied to a cost model. Customer provided throughput target (~25 req/s) and 80ms P95 latency target. We discussed sessionization, TTL policy, and tradeoffs between small batches + low latency vs larger batches for unit cost.

Topics covered:
- Current stack and constraints
- Sessionization vs global cache patterns
- Lookahead (prefix) caching heuristics and TTLs
- Batch sizing, concurrency, and autoscale knobs
- Cost-sensitivity spreadsheet walk-through
- Next steps and pilot scope

Discussed root-cause hypotheses (driver preemption, async kernel scheduling, prefetch throttling), short-term mitigations (aggressive kvcache backoff, request sharding, smaller batch windows), ...
text: Deep technical review of recurring CUDA RTC lockups causing GPU stalls and KV-cache saturation during mixed seq throughput. Discussed root-cause hypotheses (driver preemption, async kernel scheduling, prefetch throttling), short-term mitigations (aggressive kvcache backoff, request sharding, smaller batch windows), and long-term fixes (runtime watchdog, kernel selector tweaks, driver knob matrix). Agreed next steps: Redwood to run instrumented repro with tuned driver/RT settings, share trace collection script, Neptune to provide small repro trace and priority logs, align on pilot test window.

title: Sprint 4: Latency & Throughput Gap Review + Fallback Playbook — AstridCorp POC
preview: Week 4 readout: Redwood matches median latency vs incumbent on short, variable prompts but shows higher tail (p95/p99) under burst and cold-cache conditions. Throughput (tokens/sec) is comparable when batching is tuned; core gap appears in cache warmup and routing under multi-tenant noise. Agreed playbook: run stead...
text: Week 4 readout: Redwood matches median latency vs incumbent on short, variable prompts but shows higher tail (p95/p99) under burst and cold-cache conditions. Throughput (tokens/sec) is comparable when batching is tuned; core gap appears in cache warmup and routing under multi-tenant noise. Agreed playbook: run steady-state warm-cache head-to-head, enable prefix caching, tweak batch window for medium prompts, and schedule dedicated-slot run. Security docs and routing workbook to be shared.

Next: pricing + SOC2/KMS docs + burst test.

text:
Header:
Date: 2026-06-16
Start: 09:32 UTC
Duration: 58 minutes
Attendees: Jordan (Redwood AE), Priya (Redwood SE), Enzo (Redwood Perf), Mark (Helios), Sophia (Helios), Rita (Helios Security)

Summary:
Short deep dive focused on GPU runtime behavior when CUDA graphs are enabled, fallback behavior when graphs are unstable, kernel-path selection between TensorRT-LLM and vLLM backends, and the interaction with persistent KV cache and batching. Customer shared production traces and constraints: 8k monthly users, strict 95th percentile latency SLO of 220ms for short chat flows, and on-prem VPC deployment preference for IP-restricted environments.

Topics discussed:
- CUDA graph stability, warm vs cold runs
- Kernel selection heuristics: fused attention ops, rotary/rope handling, flash attention variants
- TRT-LLM vs vLLM: memory layout, workspace, and prepacking costs
- Persistent KV cache ...
